explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode and simultaneously the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) and the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then in the next state (X), an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause) shall begin. This directly translates the given natural language requirement, ensuring that the inspiratory pause is initiated immediately following the specified conditions.

The property states that globally, if the system is operating in PSV Mode, and it is the end of an inspiratory phase, and the Inspiratory Pause has been set by the GUI, then an Inspiratory Pause must start in the very next state. This accurately captures the conditional requirement for the Inspiratory Pause to commence immediately after the specified conditions are met.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode and the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) while the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then in the very next state (X), an Inspiratory Pause shall start (inspiratoryPause). This directly captures the requirement for an immediate start of the inspiratory pause under the specified conditions within PSV mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is in 'PSV Mode', then it must be the case that whenever the 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' occurs AND the 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is set by the GUI, an 'inspiratoryPause' shall start. The 'inspiratoryPause' starting is represented by the atomic proposition 'inspiratoryPause' becoming true in the same state where the conditions are met, capturing the immediate consequence indicated by 'shall start'. The implication ensures that this behavior is conditional on being in PSV Mode.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (PSVMode), then whenever the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton) at that exact moment, it must be the case that an Inspiratory Pause starts in the very next state (X inspiratoryPause). This captures the immediate consequence implied by 'shall start' and the contextual dependency on 'PSV Mode' and the two simultaneous conditions at 'the end of an inspiration phase'.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (PSVMode) AND the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), THEN an Inspiratory Pause shall start (X inspiratoryPause). The 'X' (Next) operator is used to capture the notion of 'shall start', indicating that the Inspiratory Pause becomes active in the very next state after all the conditions in the antecedent are met. The PSVMode is included as a conjunct in the antecedent as the requirement is specific to this mode.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode ('PSVMode'), and the inspiratory phase ends ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd'), and the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI ('inspiratoryPauseButton'), then it must be true that in the next state (X), an Inspiratory Pause ('inspiratoryPause') starts. This accurately captures the conditional requirement for the inspiratory pause to begin immediately following the specified conditions.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is operating in PSV Mode (PSVMode), then whenever the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause) must start in the next state (X). This directly captures the 'At the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause shall start' requirement within the PSV Mode context.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) Mode, then whenever the inspiratory phase ends and the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (Graphical User Interface) at the same moment, an Inspiratory Pause must start in the very next time step. This directly translates 'At the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause shall start' by using 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' for the event, 'inspiratoryPauseButton' for the condition, and 'X inspiratoryPause' for the consequence, all under the 'PSVMode' context.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is operating in PSV mode (PSVMode), and it is the end of an inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then in the next state (X), an Inspiratory Pause must start (inspiratoryPause). This captures the immediate causal relationship described in the requirement.
